data.title: Circle shares indicated to open as much as 42% above IPO price - report
data.description:
Circle Internet Group (NYSE:CRCL) stock is indicated to debut at $42-$44 per share on Thursday, representing a 36%-42% premium relative to the $31 initial public offering price, Bloomberg reported.

The stablecoin issuer and some of its backers raised $1.1B in the offering, selling 34M shares for $31 each. That was upsized from the previous marketed range of $27-$28 after being boosted once before amid strong demand.

Shares are slated to start trading Thursday on the New York Stock Exchange.

Circle (NYSE:CRCL) is the issuer of USD Coin (USDC-USD), the second-largest stablecoin by market cap.

A stablecoin is a type of crypto designed to maintain a stable value by being pegged to a real-world asset like the U.S. dollar or gold.

data.title: Harmony Biosciences, Lupin reach agreement on Wakix generic launch
data.description:
• Lupin can launch a generic version of Harmony Biosciences' (NASDAQ:HRMY) Wakix (pitolisant) in January 2030 under a settlement agreement.
  • The agreement resolves patent infringement litigation filed by Harmony against Lupin regarding its ANDA for generic Wakix, a treatment for excessive daytime sleepiness.
  • Harmony noted that it is currently working on new formulations of pitolisant with utility patents that could provide exclusivity through 2044.

data.title: ScottsMiracle-Gro reaffirms fiscal 2025 guidance; shares up over 10%
data.description:
• Shares of The Scotts Miracle-Gro Company jumped over 10% on Thursday after the company reaffirmed its full fiscal year 2025 guidance. 
  • The company expects U.S. consumer net sales low single-digit growth, excluding non-repeat sales for AeroGarden and bulk raw material sales. 
  • The company expects non-GAAP adjusted gross margin of approximately 30 percent and non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA of $570 million to $590 million and free cash flow of approximately $250 million.
  • The company also projects interest expense to be approximately $30 million lower than the prior year compared to its previous estimate of a $15 million to $20 million decrease, and it projects diluted share count increase to be approximately 1 million compared to its previous estimate of 2 million.
  • The company expects non-GAAP adjusted earnings per diluted share to be at least $3.50 vs. consensus of $3.47, a 53% increase versus prior year.
  • Chief Financial Officer Mark Scheiwer said, “The strong consumer takeaway, along with the pace of retailer replenishment thus far in the third quarter, solidifies our confidence in the full-year guidance. Delivering on our adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow targets will enable us to exit 2025 with a significantly improved debt position that will put us on a path to realize our goal of getting leverage below 3.5 by the end of fiscal 2027.”
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Qualcomm completes acquisition of Autotalks after overcoming FTC roadblocks
data.description:
Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) has completed its acquisition of Autotalks, a developer of direct vehicle-to-everything communication solutions, after overcoming roadblocks from the U.S. Federal Trade Commission's Bureau of Competition when it tried to acquire the company last year.

Autotalks' V2X technologies are used to reduce collisions by enabling vehicles to communicate directly with nearby vehicles and the surrounding environment. The financial details of the acquisition were not disclosed.

"We've been investing in V2X technologies since 2017, recognizing their potential to improve road safety," said Nakul Duggal, general manager of Automotive and Industrial & Embedded IoT at Qualcomm. "This acquisition is a strategic step in our ongoing efforts, advancing V2X solutions further for connected and automated vehicles, while driving global deployment, and strengthening our Snapdragon Digital Chassis product portfolio."

"The combination of our expertise and industry-leading V2X products with Qualcomm Technologies' global automotive footprint and strong momentum among automakers positions us well to accelerate V2X developments and adoption across the globe," added Hagai Zyss, former CEO of Autotalks, who is now vice president of Product Management at Qualcomm. 

Last year, the FTC probed Qualcomm's proposed acquisition of Autotalks, prompting Qualcomm to back out of the deal.

"The termination of this proposed transaction will preserve vigorous innovation and price competition in the market for vehicle-to-everything chipsets and related products used in automotive safety systems," said Henry Liu in March 2024, who was then director of the FTC's Bureau of Competition.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow look for direction as China President Xi Jinping and Trump hold call
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages look for direction on Thursday, as reports suggested that Chinese President Xi Jinping and President Donald Trump held talks on the phone.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was near even, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was basically flat, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was bear even at 4.35%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) advanced 1 basis point to 3.87%.

Media reports suggested that Xi Jinping and Trump held a telephonic conversation, mostly related to tariffs and trade. 

The U.S. trade deficit narrowed sharply in April as imports plunged.

The initial jobless claims rose more than expected in the past week. 

Productivity growth was revised lower in the final Q1 estimates, and labor costs extended their climb.

The U.S. services sector leads in May job cuts, according to the Challenger Report.

Wall Street on Wednesday closed little changed, while bonds rallied after private hiring in May reached its lowest level in over two years.

"Markets put in a strong performance yesterday, after a weak batch of US data led to a massive rally for U.S.Treasuries, which in turn supported risk assets," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Of course, even with the rally, the data still raised fears that the U.S. economy lost steam into May. That started off with the ADP’s report of private payrolls, which was the softest in over two years, Allen added. 

The main event in terms of labor data—the monthly employment reading—will land on Friday.

data.title: Silver soars to 13-year high as safe-haven investor interest extends beyond gold
data.description:
Silver futures surged Thursday to their highest in more than 13 years, as investors broaden demand for the safety of precious metals beyond gold.

Front-month Comex silver (XAGUSD:CUR) for July delivery +4% to $36.03/oz, after rising to as high as $36.27, the best level since February 2012, while gold futures are little changed for the day.

Silver's power move lifts gains above 20% during the past year, although still lagging the move in gold, which has jumped 44% in the last 12 months, as the expanded tariff war bolstered its appeal as a haven and central banks maintained elevated levels of buying.

Silver mining shares are riding the metal's wave in Thursday's trading: Pan American Silver (PAAS) +12.8%, Aya Gold & Silver (OTCQX:AYASF) +11.9%, Endeavour Silver (EXK) +11.7%, Fortuna Mining (FSM) +11%, Hecla Mining (HL) +10.5%, Vizsla Silver (VZLA) +9%, Coeur Mining (CDE) +6.3%, Silvercorp Metals (SVM) +5.8%, MAG Silver (MAG) +5.4%, Avino Silver & Gold (ASM) +5.4%, Fresnillo (OTCPK:FNLPF) +4.7%, Buenaventura (BVN) +3.9%.

Referring to the gold-silver ratio, which indicates how many ounces of silver can be bought with one ounce of gold, analysts say some ratio trading may be occurring since the ratio has dipped below the 100 level, following silver's relative underperformance vs. gold because of economic concerns, since 70% of silver usage is industrial.

Silver also has been helped by significant inflows into silver-backed ETFs, with holdings up by 2.2M oz on Wednesday, according to data compiled by Bloomberg.

A recent survey from the Silver Institute estimated that the supply of silver was ~15% lower than demand in 2024 and projected another deficit in 2025.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (NYSEARCA:SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: AstroNova reiterates $160M–$165M revenue outlook for FY26 as new products and cost actions gain traction
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: AstroNova (ALOT) Q1 2026

Management View

  • Gregory A. Woods, President and CEO, emphasized AstroNova’s strategy to drive long-term revenue growth and improve profitability, leveraging its position in the data visualization market, stating, "Our goal is to leverage our technologies and market position and our streamlined organization to deliver stronger growth and earnings with more predictable performance."
  • Woods detailed three strategic drivers: rapid transition in the Aerospace segment to ToughWriter printers, the launch of next-generation product identification (Product ID) solutions, and streamlining operations through headcount reductions and restructuring. Woods highlighted, "We delivered double-digit growth in both segments and increased consolidated adjusted operating income by 13.5% year-over-year."
  • Launches of the QL-425, QL-435, and AJ-800 printers were completed ahead of schedule, and the company is planning two more next-generation launches in Q2. Early traction from these products with both new and existing customers was noted.
  • Renewed a $10 million multiyear ToughWriter contract with a prime defense contractor, with $1.7 million of revenue expected in the current fiscal year. Woods reported, "We expect our margin profile and aftermarket sales to strengthen as the rollout of our new Product ID solutions gain market acceptance and our ToughWriter transition continues to advance."
  • Thomas D. DeByle, VP, Treasurer & CFO, stated: "First quarter revenue of $37.7 million grew 14.4% year-over-year and 0.9% sequentially. 83% of the quarter's revenue was recurring."

Outlook

  • AstroNova reiterated full-year fiscal 2026 guidance for revenue in the range of $160 million to $165 million and adjusted EBITDA margin between 8.5% and 9.5%.
  • Woods maintained, "We are reiterating our guidance for the full year of fiscal 2026. We expect to deliver full year revenue of $160 million to $165 million, a 7% year-over-year increase at the midpoint and adjusted EBITDA margin in the range of 8.5% to 9.5%."
  • No explicit changes to guidance figures from the previous quarter were noted.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q1 2026 was $37.7 million, reflecting a 14.4% year-over-year increase and a 0.9% sequential rise.
  • 83% of revenue was recurring, with Product Identification up 13.8% and Aerospace up 16.8% year-over-year.
  • Net loss was ($0.4 million) or (-$0.05) per share, with adjusted net income of $0.4 million or $0.05 per share.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $3.1 million, up 27.6% compared with the prior year and 28% sequentially.
  • Gross profit was $12.7 million, or 33.6% of sales; adjusted gross profit was $13.1 million, or 34.6% of sales.
  • Product ID operating income stood at $2.8 million (10.6% of sales), while Aerospace operating income was $2.8 million (24.2% of sales).
  • Backlog ended at $25.5 million, down $2.8 million year-over-year, attributed to clearing delayed shipments.
  • The company reported $4.4 million in cash provided by operations and ended the quarter with $12.6 million in total liquidity.

Q&A

  • There were no analyst questions during the call. The operator confirmed, “There are no questions at this time.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • The absence of analyst questions suggests a neutral to slightly positive sentiment from the analyst community, with no immediate pressing concerns raised.
  • Management tone was confident throughout the prepared remarks. Woods stated, "We are confident in our ability to deliver long-term shareholder value through the focused execution of our strategy" and "We believe that this quarter reflected a positive turning point in our business."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment appeared more constructive, with management emphasizing execution and momentum.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management maintained revenue and EBITDA margin guidance at $160 million to $165 million and 8.5% to 9.5%, unchanged from the previous quarter.
  • Strategic focus shifted from integration challenges and restructuring in Q4 2025 to accelerated product launches and early traction in Q1 2026.
  • Analyst engagement remained low in both quarters.
  • Key metric changes included a return to double-digit growth in both business segments and higher recurring revenue.
  • Management’s confidence increased, with Woods calling Q1 2026 a “positive turning point” versus the prior quarter’s references to integration and performance challenges.

Risks and Concerns

  • Woods acknowledged, “We still have more work to do...we expect our margin profile and aftermarket sales to strengthen as the rollout of our new Product ID solutions gain market acceptance and our ToughWriter transition continues to advance.”
  • Management cited backlog reduction due to clearing delayed shipments, but expects benefits from new product launches and increasing aerospace build rates.
  • Tariff impacts were described as negligible so far, with mitigation strategies including inventory management, global sourcing flexibility, and price adjustments.
  • The company continues to manage cost structure and liquidity, aiming to reduce leverage ratio to approximately 2x.

Final Takeaway

AstroNova’s first quarter of fiscal 2026 demonstrated momentum in both its Aerospace and Product ID segments, highlighted by strong recurring revenue, successful execution of product launches, and the acceleration of cost reductions. The company reaffirmed its full-year revenue and margin targets and remains focused on driving growth through new technologies, streamlined operations, and expanded customer relationships. Management’s tone reflected increased confidence in the business outlook and the effectiveness of recent strategic actions, positioning AstroNova to deliver on its long-term growth and profitability objectives.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Ken Griffin says policy uncertainty, fiscal picture are key worries
data.description:
Billionaire Ken Griffin, founder and chief executive of hedge fund and market maker Citadel, said U.S. trade policy and federal spending are two of the biggest sources of uncertainty facing investors.

Speaking at the Forbes Iconoclast Summit in New York, Griffin said the Trump administration’s tariff policies are disrupting the global economy in ways that are ultimately harmful for Americans.

““Unfortunately, the last few months have been characterized by a set of policy decisions that have rendered a great deal of uncertainty, have called into question American exceptionalism,” he said.

There’s still hope that the Trump administration will pivot back toward the pro-growth policies that characterized its first few weeks, he said. In December, Griffin said he voted for Trump.

Another key concern is the effect of federal deficits on everything from U.S. consumer spending to the value of the U.S. dollar.

“We really need to get our fiscal house in order,” he said.

data.title: Owens & Minor cancels Rotech buyout over regulatory issues
data.description:
Owens & Minor (NYSE:OMI) on Thursday announced an agreement to terminate its previously announced $1.36B acquisition of privately held home medical equipment provider Rotech Healthcare Holdings (ROTK) due to regulatory issues.

Concurrently, Glen Allen, Virginia-based healthcare solution provider announced plans to redeem $1B of notes issued in April to fund the acquisition. Other financing options, including term loan commitments, will also be terminated, the company said.

Owens & Minor (NYSE:OMI) has already paid $80M to Rotech under the terms of the all-cash merger deal, which the company previously expected to close by the end of 2024 in a bid to expand its footprint in the home-based care market.

Noting that the U.S. regulators have declined to clear the transaction, CEO Edward Pesicka said, “For many months, our teammates, along with the Rotech team, have worked tirelessly in cooperation with the Federal Trade Commission to close this transaction.”

“The path to obtain regulatory clearance for this merger proved unviable in terms of time, expense, and opportunity.”

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: PVH, MDB, TOPS, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Thursday after initial jobless claims rose more than expected last week, fueling concerns about the labor market. Investors also remained cautious amid lingering uncertainty over the Trump administration’s trade policies, with focus shifting to Friday’s closely watched nonfarm payrolls report.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • MongoDB (NASDAQ:MDB) +15% – Shares rallied after the database company posted better-than-expected Q1 results, raised its full-year guidance, and expanded its share buyback program. For Q2, MongoDB expects revenue of $548M–$553M and adjusted EPS of $0.62–$0.66, above analyst estimates of $549.28M and $0.58, respectively. Full-year revenue is now forecast at $2.25B–$2.29B, up from $2.24B–$2.28B, while adjusted EPS is projected at $2.94–$3.12, significantly above the previous $2.44–$2.62 range and the $2.65 consensus.
  • Verint Systems (NASDAQ:VRNT) +21% – Shares surged after the company delivered better-than-expected Q1 results, highlighted by a 24% Y/Y increase in AI annual recurring revenue (AI ARR), which includes its AI-powered software offerings. Verint expects AI ARR to grow more than 20% for the full year, an acceleration from last year. The company is targeting 8% ARR growth overall, and with expanding cash contribution margins, it anticipates a 12% increase in free cash flow for the year.
  • Five Below (NASDAQ:FIVE) +5% – Shares gained after the specialty discount retailer reported a top- and bottom-line beat for Q1, with comparable sales rising 7.1%, ahead of the 6.4% consensus. The company opened 55 new stores during the quarter, bringing the total to 1,826 locations across 44 states, a 13.8% increase Y/Y. For Q2, Five Below guided net sales between $975M and $995M, ahead of the $958.3M consensus. Adjusted EPS is expected in the range of $0.50 to $0.62 (midpoint $0.56 vs. estimate $0.58), with guidance factoring in the impact of tariffs. The company expects 7% to 9% growth in comparable sales and plans to open approximately 30 net new stores. Separately, Five Below announced that CFO Kristy Chipman will step down for personal reasons. Current COO Ken Bull, who previously served as CFO for over a decade, will assume the finance chief duties on an interim basis.

Biggest stock losers

  • TOP Ships (NYSE:TOPS) -12% – Shares fell after a 44% rally in the previous session, as the company announced plans to spin off two Suezmax tanker vessels into a new publicly traded entity, Rubico. Rubico, currently a subsidiary of TOP Ships, will become an independent Nasdaq-listed company, holding the M/T Eco Malibu and M/T Eco West Coast—modern, high-spec, fuel-efficient tankers. As part of the spin-off, TOP Ships will distribute 100% of Rubico’s common shares to shareholders and warrant holders of record as of June 16, 2025. Rubico also expects to raise $1.5M via a private placement at $20 per share.
  • PVH Corp. (NYSE:PVH) -18 — Shares tumbled after the apparel maker slashed its full-year and Q2 earnings guidance due to tariff-related pressures, overshadowing stronger-than-expected Q1 results. For FY2025, PVH now expects non-GAAP EPS of $10.75 to $11.00, down from the prior range of $12.40 to $12.75. The new midpoint of $10.88 comes in below the consensus of $11.08. The revision reflects a $1.05 per share unmitigated impact from tariffs, partially offset by mitigation efforts and a $0.10 benefit from foreign exchange. For Q2, the company projects revenue growth in the low single digits Y/Y (flat to slightly up on a constant currency basis) and sees EPS in the range of $1.85 to $2.00, compared to $3.01 a year earlier and below the $2.06 consensus. The Q2 outlook includes a ~$0.20 per share hit from tariffs, with minimal FX impact expected.
